post.author: Story Makers
post.caption: 8042615Story Makers · FollowStory Makers · Original audioThe cowboy stood on the wooden platform, grinning beneath his black hat while the crowd watched from the bleachers.The young woman stood below him in a faded olive dress, dust around her boots, her eyes lowered so no one could see they were wet.He lifted the microphone.“Sing… and I’ll marry you.”The crowd laughed.Some cheered.Some whistled.The woman’s hands tightened at her sides, but she didn’t run.The cowboy smiled wider.“What’s wrong? Afraid they’ll hear the truth?”She looked toward the old guitarist sitting near the arena fence.He gave her one small nod.So she walked to the microphone.Every step through the dirt felt heavier than the last.The old man began to play a soft, sad melody.The crowd quieted, waiting for her to fail.Then she sang one line.Raw.Broken.Beautiful.The cowboy’s smile vanished.His face went pale.He stepped down from the platform.“Who taught you that song?”The old guitarist looked away, tears in his eyes.👉 Part 2 in the comments See less ::-webkit-scrollbar { -webkit-appearance: none; width: 7px; } ::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb { border-radius: 4px; background: var(--scroll-thumb); }9.3K10410311K12185
